commit.message:
Fix flaky rx-state multiInstance test by yielding on CONFLICT retry (#8116)

* Initial plan

* fix: yield to event loop on CONFLICT retry in RxState._triggerWrite()

In multiInstance mode, the CONFLICT retry loop in _triggerWrite() could
spin in a tight loop without yielding to the event loop, preventing
cross-instance change events from propagating and updating the
_lastIdQuery cache. This caused the 'write with two states at once'
test to intermittently time out.

Adding promiseWait(0) after catching CONFLICT gives the event loop a
chance to process incoming events before retrying.

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Daniel Meyer <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Forward browser uncaught errors and unhandled rejections to terminal in CI (#8111)

* Initial plan

* Forward browser uncaught errors and unhandled rejections to terminal in CI

Add window.addEventListener('unhandledrejection') and
window.addEventListener('error') handlers in the browser path of
init.test.ts. These log error details via console.error, which Karma's
browserConsoleLogOptions forwards to the terminal. This ensures errors
are visible in CI logs instead of just showing a timeout disconnect.

* Guard window.addEventListener with typeof window check for Deno compatibility

Deno is not Node but also does not have window defined, so the
window.addEventListener calls need a typeof window !== 'undefined'
guard to avoid ReferenceError in Deno test runs.

* Fix codespell: re-use -> reuse in performance.ts

Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix stale par alias references and update comments

- views/parent_home.php: Remove par.relationship_type and par.id
  references from COALESCE (table alias no longer exists)
- views/parent_home.php: Update comment to remove dual-table mention
- views/manage_athletes.php: Update comment to remove dual-table mention

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Replace all parent_athlete_relationships references with managed_athletes

Migrate all PHP code from the legacy parent_athlete_relationships table
to use the managed_athletes table exclusively for parent-child relationships.

Changes across 28 files:
- Simple access checks: use managed_athletes WHERE parent_id = ? AND athlete_id = ?
- Child listings: INNER JOIN managed_athletes ON parent_id = ?
- Child detection: managed_athletes WHERE athlete_id = ? AND parent_id IS NOT NULL
- Remove dual-table fallback patterns (check PAR then MA)
- Remove LEFT JOIN + COALESCE patterns across both tables
- Remove UNION queries spanning both tables
- Remove INSERT into parent_athlete_relationships in process_parent_invitations
- Remove parent_athlete_relationships from row_level_security config
- Update parameter counts where bind variables were reduced
- Update outdated comment in process_create_athlete.php

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add Journey log docs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add FPL API client package with typed models and tests

Implement Phase 1.2: HTTP client for the Fantasy Premier League API at
internal/fpl/. Includes typed structs for 5 endpoints (bootstrap, event
status, H2H standings, manager history, manager picks), a pagination
helper, table-driven tests with httptest, and skip-by-default live API
tests for manual validation. Wire client into main.go with a startup
connectivity check.

commit.message:
test: add shared fixtures and improve test infrastructure (#105)

- Add project-wide tests/conftest.py with common path fixtures,
  seed reset, mocks, and skip marks (skip_if_missing_testdata,
  skip_if_no_gpu)
- Implement --use-gpu pytest option via pytest_addoption and
  use_gpu fixture so the gpu_compatible marker is functional
- Guard all CUDA calls in reset_seed behind torch.cuda.is_available()
  to prevent failures in CPU-only environments
- Add CLI-specific tests/mussel/cli/conftest.py with test_data_path_cli
  alias fixture
- Register gpu_compatible marker in pyproject.toml

Addresses review comments:
- No unused heavy imports at collection time
- --use-gpu flag properly registered via pytest_addoption
- CUDA API calls guarded for CPU-only torch installs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: initial implementation of KG testing harness

- 4 graph types: Simple, Hierarchical, Multi, WeightedGraph
- 5 multi-turn test scenarios (54 total turns)
- Ollama client with qwen3.5:4b + thinking token stripping
- Context manager with sliding window (forces KG use)
- KG-augmented agent with label/tag search and neighbor expansion
- Test runner with keyword accuracy evaluation
- Markdown reporter for per-run and summary reports
- Research matrix builder for full parameter sweep
- Initial quick test: 98.3% accuracy across 54 turns

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: KG-Testing

commit.message:
Run FoundationDB tests via Docker container instead of local server install (#8109)

* Initial plan

* Use Docker container for FoundationDB server instead of local installation

- Modified scripts/install-foundationdb.sh to only install the FDB client library
- Created scripts/start-foundationdb-docker.sh to start FDB in Docker with host networking, copy cluster file, configure database, and verify readiness
- Added foundationdb:start and foundationdb:stop npm scripts in package.json
- Updated .github/workflows/main.yml to use Docker container instead of locally installed server
- Updated docs-src/docs/rx-storage-foundationdb.md with Docker setup instructions

Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address code review feedback: fix comment and remove unused variable

Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix permission denied when copying FDB cluster file in CI

docker cp cannot write directly to /etc/foundationdb/ (root-owned).
Copy to /tmp first, then sudo cp to the final destination.

Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>

* Move Docker section to end of FoundationDB docs page

Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: WebAuthn registration for iOS passkeys (Face ID)

- Set ResidentKey to 'preferred' for discoverable credential support
  (required for iOS Passwords/Keychain passkey storage)
- Auto-detect request origin and add to RPOrigins dynamically so
  the origin validation passes when accessing from mobile devices
- Support comma-separated WEBAUTHN_ORIGIN for multiple origins
- Pre-read request body to prevent double-consumption issue
- Include transports and authenticatorAttachment in attestation response
- Add structured error logging for WebAuthn ceremonies

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: make PWA gallery menu a popover instead of pushing content down

menu absolutely so it floats over the content as a popover rather than
shifting the gallery view downward when opened.

commit.message:
Add minimal wp-env PHPUnit application test scaffolding and developer-oriented docs (#178)

* Initial plan

* feat: add wp-env phpunit bootstrap docs and ci snippet

Co-authored-by: JUVOJustin <30726576+JUVOJustin@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: address review feedback on phpunit bootstrap docs

Co-authored-by: JUVOJustin <30726576+JUVOJustin@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: restructure testing guide with starlight steps and first test flow

Co-authored-by: JUVOJustin <30726576+JUVOJustin@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: rewrite testing guide as developer-oriented authoring walkthrough

Co-authored-by: JUVOJustin <30726576+JUVOJustin@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: tighten test examples in application testing guide

Co-authored-by: JUVOJustin <30726576+JUVOJustin@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: harden phpunit setup and activate CI test job

- Activate test-application CI job (was commented out)
- Remove deprecated phpunit.xml.dist attributes
- Add tests/php/ExampleTest.php as working smoke test
- Use container phpunit directly in test:php script (no Composer wrapper)
- Restore yoast/phpunit-polyfills as explicit dep (WordPress test bootstrap requirement)
- Remove phpunit/phpunit and wp-phpunit/wp-phpunit (provided by container and polyfills)
- Simplify bootstrap.php: drop non-wp-env fallback, fail fast with clear message
- Update testing docs: wp-env-exclusive constraint, tests-cli rationale, configuration notes

* ci: run application tests after static analysis passes

Exit early benefit: avoid spinning up Docker/wp-env when PHPStan
or lint already failed. Cache keys still resolve via needs: setup.

* docs: update github-actions doc with test-application job and stage structure

* chore: fix gitignore and distignore for phpunit artifacts

- Add phpunit.xml and .phpunit.result.cache to .gitignore
- Add phpunit.xml to .distignore
- Remove stale Cypress test entries from .gitignore

* docs: add plugin dependencies section to testing guide

* feat: add application-testing skill for writing PHPUnit tests

* chore: remove redundant namespace lookup guidance from testing skill

* docs: add application-testing skill to work-with-ai, add factory rollback note to testing

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: JUVOJustin <30726576+JUVOJustin@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Justin Vogt <mail@justin-vogt.com>
